text: Synthesis of azabicyclo[2.1.1]hexanes (aza-BCHs) and bicyclo[1.1.1]pentanes (BCPs) via “scaffold hopping” triggered by single-atom editing: Access to high sp3-molecular scaffolds of high relevance for medicinal chemistry The synthesis of high sp3-rich molecular frameworks is of high relevance in medicinal chemistry to enhance drug candidate pharmacokinetics. Azabicyclo[2.1.1]hexanes (aza-BCHs) and bicyclo[1.1.1]pentanes (BCPs) are known bioisosteres for pyrrolidines and phenyl rings. Herein we report an efficient “scaffold hopping” method that enables the interconversion of aza-BCHs and BCPs by means of single-atom skeletal editing. Our modular approach to aza-BCHs and BCPs employs an intramolecular visible-light-mediated [2+2] photocycloaddition of secondary enamides to give multifunctionalized BCHs, which upon treatment with anomeric amides leads to the desired BCPs. The nitrogen atom in the aza-BCHs serves as a temporary linchpin to direct the formation of the strained bicyclic core, which is removed in a traceless fashion. This method is applicable to a wide range of aryl and heteroaryl substituents and allows for the efficient synthesis of 1,2- and 1,2,4-substituted BCPs that are challenging to obtain by conventional propellane-based strain-release methods. We also exemplified the practical applicability of our protocol by performing a gram-scale flow photochemical synthesis.
